title: UK House Price Index for September 2025
body: "The September data shows:   on average, house prices have fallen -0.6% since August 2025 there has been an annual price rise of 2.6% which makes the average property in the UK valued at £272,000   England  In England the September data shows, on average, house prices fell by -0.8% since August 2025. The annual price rise of 2% takes the average property value to £293,000.  The regional data for England indicates that:   Yorkshire and the Humber experienced the only monthly increase, with a rise of 0.3% the South East and the North East saw the biggest monthly price falls, with movements of -1.2% Yorkshire and the Humber experienced the greatest annual price rise, up by 4.5% London was the only region not to experience an annual rise, with a fall of -1.8%   Price change by region for England     Region Average price September 2025 Annual change % since September 2024 Monthly change % since August 2025     East Midlands £243,000 3.7 -0.6   East of England £341,000 2.3 -0.6   London £556,000 -1.8 -1.1   North East £162,000 3.5 -1.2   North West £215,000 3.4 -0.9   South East £384,000 0.9 -1.2   South West £307,000 0.9 -0.9   West Midlands £249,000 2.6 -0.5   Yorkshire and the Humber £208,000 4.5 0.3     Repossession sales by volume for England  The lowest number of repossession sales in July 2025 was in the East of England.  The highest number of repossession sales in July 2025 was in London.     Repossession sales July 2025     East Midlands 5   East of England 3   London 24   North East 12   North West 20   South East 8   South West 4   West Midlands 11   Yorkshire and the Humber 12   England 99     Average price by property type for England     Property type September 2025 September  2024 Difference %     Detached £477,000 £466,000 2.2   Semi-detached £289,000 £280,000 3.3   Terraced £244,000 £238,000 2.4   Flat/maisonette £223,000 £226,000 -1.1   All £293,000 £288,000 2     Funding and buyer status for England     Transaction type Average price September 2025 Annual price change % since September 2024 Monthly price change % since August 2025     Cash £279,000 1.4 -0.7   Mortgage £299,000 2.3 -0.8   First-time buyer £246,000 2.2 -0.8   Former owner occupier £356,000 1.7 -0.7     Building status for England     Building status* Average price July 2025 Annual price change % since July 2024 Monthly price change % since June 2025     New build £414,000 16.4 4.4   Existing resold property £288,000 2.2 0.4     *Figures for the 2 most recent months are not being published because there are not enough new build transactions to give a meaningful result.  London  London shows, on average, house prices fell by 1.1% since August 2025. House prices have shown an annual price decrease of 1.8% meaning the average price of a property is £556,000.  Average price by property type for London     Property type September 2025 September 2024 Difference %     Detached £1,131,000 £1,170,000 -3.4   Semi-detached £710,000 £711,000 -0.2   Terraced £634,000 £637,000 -0.6   Flat/maisonette £441,000 £454,000 -2.9   All £556,000 £567,000 -1.8     Funding and buyer status for London     Transaction type Average price September 2025 Annual price change % since September 2024 Monthly price change % since August 2025...
